mainfeature_subtitle: Cfluent is an AI communication companion designed for AAC. It generates editable suggestions from live context and works alongside Cboard.
morefeature_subtitle1: Get natural phrase options based on whatРђЎs being said around youРђћthen tap to speak, edit, or ignore. You stay in control of every message.
morefeature_subtitle2: Designed to reduce effort: fewer taps, faster replies, and suggestions that match your voice and vocabulary. Compatible with access methods like touch, switch, and eye tracking (where supported by your AAC setup).
morefeature_subtitle3: We minimize data collection and prioritize secure processing. Pilot deployments can be configured for strict privacy needs (e.g., clinical or school settings).
benefit_title: Reduce communication lag and socialize more
testi_subtitle: Clinicians, educators, and AAC users testing faster conversations with Cfluent.
faq_subtitle: Questions about privacy, pilots, accessibility, or integrations? YouРђЎll find answers here.

faq_1.a: Cfluent uses AI to listen to the conversation around you (with consent) and generates real-time phrase suggestions based on the context. It integrates with Cboard, allowing AAC users to quickly select, edit, or ignore suggestions to keep conversations flowing naturally.

faq_2.q: Is Cfluent compatible with all AAC devices?
faq_2.a: Cfluent is designed to work with a wide range of AAC devices, but compatibility may vary depending on the specific device and setup. It integrates seamlessly with Cboard, and we are continuously working to expand support for additional AAC tools and platforms.

faq_3.a: In the pilot phase, Cfluent supports English, Spanish, and Mandarin. We plan to expand language support based on user feedback and demand.

faq_4.q: How does Cfluent ensure user privacy?
faq_4.a: User privacy is our top priority. Cfluent minimizes data collection and processes all data securely. Users have full control over their data, and pilot deployments can be configured to meet strict privacy requirements, such as those in clinical or educational settings.

faq_5.q: How can I join the Cfluent pilot?
faq_5.a: You can join the Cfluent pilot by signing up for our waitlist. We are currently accepting applications from AAC users, clinicians, educators, and organizations interested in testing Cfluent. Join the waitlist to receive updates and be among the first to experience our real-time AI communication companion.
